crime.value: Murder of Wilson Sharp found dead in bed, discovered after a break-in at the Dainsbury house
crime.quote: Allie cried, 'My God, Wil's dead!' and she ran to the phone and dialed 911.

victim.value: Wilson Sharp, described as a go-getter and hustler, found dead in bed
victim.quote: He was a hustler, that man. Maybe I'm a little slow, but death just didn't occur to me at first.

closed_world.value: The Colonial house where the murder occurred and break-in took place
closed_world.quote: she saw the forced rear mud room door

investigator.value: Detective Gertz conducting interviews with witnesses Lauren Olsen and others
investigator.quote: Gertz nodded, jotting in her notebook. 'And then what happened? Your sister went upstairs?'

red_herring.value: Initial burglary setup with Artis and Leon discovering the body creates false trail of robbery gone wrong
red_herring.quote: We don't know nothing, and we sure don't want to get messed up in no white people's troubles.

clue_fairness.value: Door lock status established fairly - locked when sister arrived, requiring key to enter
clue_fairness.quote: Why, it was locked, of course... Yes, of course she did. I remember because she dropped her keys

social_world.value: Class and racial dynamics - working class burglars afraid of white authority, Hispanic housekeeper trusted by employers
social_world.quote: the Dainsburys like my work and they know they can trust me

alibi.value: Burglars' mutual alibi of never being there, sister's account of locked door and key use
alibi.quote: We were never there, Leon. Remember that.
